ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jan-06-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jennifer L. Steneberg for Raymond James Trust, N.A., Trustee (via Zoom).

The court indicated its inclination to adhere to the 1% fair market guideline for fees, rather than approve the amount requested by the trustee. Ms. Steneberg raised no objection to the court's suggestion.

The court took the matter under submission and stated that it will issue an order with its determination of an appropriate fee based on the fair market value guideline.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Order Establishing Special Needs Trust, Authorizing Investment In Mutual Funds And Approving Trustee Fees On Account
Off Calendar Aug-17-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Steneberg, via ZOOM, for Raymond James Trust, N.A., Petitioner.

The court begins by advising counsel that allowing the removal of a trustee without cause requires court approval and subsequent order appointing a successor trustee. Counsel advises that she believed the preceding would be implied and reports that a revised order would be submitted. The court then points counsel regarding the language in the proposed order regarding compensation. Again, counsel advises that she has no objection to modifying the order. The court then points to why this trust should not be under court supervision. Counsel reports that pursuant to notes received from the appointed Examiner, a revised proposed order has been submitted electronically for lodging and that hard copies of the revised order will be delivered to the court.

The Petition to Establish Special Needs Trust, etc., is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Mary Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: B. Tso. Not reported.

Petition For Order Establishing Special Needs Trust And Approving Trustee Fees On Account
Off Calendar Mar-16-2021 Continued To May-11-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Dec-22-2020 Continued To Mar-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Steeneberg, Esq., counsel for the Raymond James Trust (appearing by video conference).

The Court recites the matters now before the Court and notes at the outset that there is no evidence that this special needs trust was ever approved by the Court. Ms. Steeneberg contends that the trust was approved by Dept. 514 as was the procedure at that time. The Court advises that it is familiar with Dept. 514 processes having presided there for some time but recalls no such procedure. Nonetheless, there is no Order authorizing the existence of this trust either in Dept 514 or Dept. 204. After further discussion, the Court determines that a Petition for Approval of Special Needs Trust must be filed.

Relatedly, the Court does not see any evidence that that the Attorney General was noticed on the filing of the Fifth Accounting. There is also concern that the Trustee has been paid on account without Court approval.

The Court continues the matter to March 16, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 and for hearing on the forthcoming Petition for Approval of Special Needs Trust. Ms. Steeneberg is to advise the Probate Filing Department that the hearing date is authorized by the Court.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Ruiz; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

Petition For Order Authorizing Purchase Of Real Property, Purchase Of An Accessible Vehicle, Payment Of Care Giver Wages And Approving Attorneys' Fees
Off Calendar Apr-12-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Steneberg for Raymond James Financial (Trustee).

Ms. Steneberg stated that the range for a new home is between $1,500,000.00 and $1,800,000.00, that they would like to receive $1,800,000.00 if they could get it, that the Client and a Real Estate Agent have looked at some properties, that they are looking for something where either a ramp or a lift can be installed, and that she has cautioned her Client not to enter into anything until they are approved for an amount. The Petition for Order Authorizing Purchase of Real Property, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Order Authorizing Purchase Of Real Property, Purchase Of An Accessible Vehicle, Payment Of Care Giver Wages And Approving Attorneys' Fees
Off Calendar Mar-18-2019 Continued To Apr-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Steneberg for Raymond James Trust, N.A. (Petitioner).

The Court stated that the issue regarding the purchase of a vehicle, caregiver payments and attorney's fees are not problematic, but there are no numbers with respect to the purchase of a property. Ms. Steneberg stated that family has not selected a specific house, that because of the housing market it is difficult for the family to secure a house, that the family does not intend to immediately make modifications to the house because the child is only five and that they intend to stay in San Francisco. Ms. Steneberg also stated that she is not sure they will be able to secure a house in that price range, that the annuity payments are for life and guaranteed for forty years, and that the money is going into the special needs trust. The Court stated that it would feel better having some quotes from a Real Estate Agent as to what properties are worth. The Petition Authorizing Purchase of Real Property, etc. is continued to April 8, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
